themes.label: Holiness
themes.summary: This chapter focuses on holiness, righteousness, purity, consecration, or being set apart. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 22 verses. Commentary leans on "But he shall let go the living bird out of the city into the open fields, and make an atoneme...". Cross references echo "Holy".

2: “These are the regulations for any diseased person at the time of their ceremonial cleansing
14: The priest is to take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
17: The priest is to put some of the oil remaining in his palm on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
18: The rest of the oil in his palm the priest shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed and make atonement for them before the Lord
19: “Then the priest is to sacrifice the sin offering and make atonement for the one to be cleansed from their uncleanness
23: “On the eighth day they must bring them for their cleansing to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting
25: He shall slaughter the lamb for the guilt offering and take some of its blood and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
28: Some of the oil in his palm he is to put on the same places he put the blood of the guilt offering—on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
29: The rest of the oil in his palm the priest shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed
31: In this way the priest will make atonement before the Lord on behalf of the one to be cleansed
32: These are the regulations for anyone who has a defiling skin disease and who cannot afford the regular offerings for their cleansing
